class Solution {
public:
    string reversePrefix(string word, char ch) {
        int pos = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); ++i) {
            if (word[i] == ch) {
                pos = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (pos == -1) return word;
        string firstPart = word.substr(0, pos + 1);
        reverse(firstPart.begin(), firstPart.end());
        return firstPart + word.substr(pos + 1);
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
#include <algorithm>
class Solution {
public:
    string reversePrefix(string word, char ch) {
        int pos = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); ++i) {
            if (word[i] == ch) {
                pos = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (pos == -1) return word;
        string firstPart = word.substr(0, pos + 1);
        std::reverse(firstPart.begin(), firstPart.end());
        return firstPart + word.substr(pos + 1);
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution first finds the first occurrence of `ch` in `word` in O(n) time. If found, it extracts the prefix up to that index, reverses it in O(n) time, and combines it with the remaining string. The algorithm is optimal for the problem constraints.
